page_content: BUFN724 Bank Management (2 Credits) Analyze and discuss readings in bank management,with primary focus on the measurement and management of risk, including credit, market, and interest rate risk. Look at the management of liquid reserves. Examine the special nature of ﬁnancial institutions, incorporating their functions, policies, services, and regulation. Study the evolving nature of the ﬁnancial services inductry, by reading the ﬁnancial press and by having outside practitioner speakers. Focus is on U.S. banks. Prerequisite: BUFN610. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Credit Only Granted for: BUFN724 or BUFN772. Formerly: BUFN772.
